title: Mark Zuckerberg returns to X after 3 years to unveil Meta's Muse Spark 1.1 AI model, developer API
snippet: Zuckerberg's first activity on the platform in over three years signaled Meta's latest push to compete directly with OpenAI and Anthropic in the rapidly expanding market for enterprise AI models.

title: Lava to launch ‘Virat’ series phones in India, Flipkart availability confirmed
snippet: Lava will soon launch its new ‘Virat’ series of smartphones in India via Flipkart. The company has confirmed that its new online-first series will feature both 4G and 5G devices

title: Meta's new AI glasses may continuously record what users see and hear: Report
snippet: Meta is developing AI-powered smart glasses that capture images and audio throughout the day.&nbsp;

title: Moto G77 Power launched in India: Check price, specifications and sale date
snippet: Motorola has launched the Moto G77 Power in India, featuring a 7,000mAh battery, 50MP camera, and MediaTek Dimensity 6400 processor.&nbsp;

title: Pixel 11 launch date revealed: Made by Google event announced for August 12
snippet: Google's next Made by Google launch event is set for August 12, 2026, in New York City.

title: ChatGPT image generation down- OpenAI says ‘implementing mitigation’
snippet: ChatGPT faced a widespread outage on Tuesday, with users reporting issues generating images and accessing past chats. OpenAI confirms it's investigating.

title: Apple now asks users before sending Apple Intelligence requests to Google Cloud
snippet: Apple's integration of Google's AI has improved features but requires sharing user data with Google Cloud.&nbsp;

title: Apple finally brings back card payments for subscriptions and App Store purchases in India
snippet: Apple has resumesdcard payments for App Store purchases in India after a four-year hiatus. Initially, only Visa and Mastercard will be accepted.&nbsp;

title: Apple could bring back its iPhone X playbook for the iPhone Ultra
snippet: Apple could be planning to use the iPhone X playbook with the iPhone Ultra by delaying the initial pre-sales and orders for the foldable iPhone.

title: New AI agent can hack systems and deploy ransomware without human help
snippet: A new report reveals that AI agents can autonomously execute ransomware attacks, evidenced by the case of JADEPUFFER, which exploited a critical vulnerability to infiltrate systems.&nbsp;

snippet: The Centre issued a notice to Meta last Wednesday, questioning the proposed username feature on WhatsApp, flagging concerns that it could materially increase incidents of online fraud, phishing, digital-arrest scams and impersonation attacks.

title: Tencent in talks to become AI startup Manus' largest shareholder
snippet: Tencent, together with Manus' original ​investors, including ZhenFund and HSG, are planning to buy the company back from Meta for no less than $2 billion, ‌said one of the sources and a third person ​briefed on ​the ⁠matter.

title: SK Hynix advisors win big fee windfall after modest SpaceX payday
snippet: The ‌fees equate ⁠to ⁠about 0.97% of the total amount raised, SK Hynix's filings ​showed, meaning bankers earned more as a percentage of deal size ​than those who worked on SpaceX's initial public offering.

title: Rubrik unveils $500 million UK investment, names London as European HQ
snippet: Rubrik was established 12 years ago to ⁠solve the problem of keeping a business up ​and running when it was targeted by a ​cyber attack, Sinha said in an interview. It listed in 2024 and currently has a market value of $17.4 billion.

title: SpaceX and AI startup wealth fuels demand for private jets
snippet: The IPO of Elon Musk's ​SpaceX, whose holdings include artificial-intelligence firm xAI, raised a record $85.7 billion for the company and generated unprecedented employee and founder wealth. Next in line for potential big IPOs are AI companies Anthropic and OpenAI.

title: Former Fed chair Ben Bernanke joins Anthropic's AI oversight trust
snippet: A Nobel ‌laureate in ⁠economic ⁠sciences, Bernanke led the Fed from 2006 to 2014, ​steering the central bank through the 2008 financial crisis.

title: Instant help burn rises; TCS kicks off Q1 earnings
snippet: Instant househelp startups are burning more cash to chase demand. This and more in today’s ETtech Morning Dispatch.

title: OpenAI's AGI deployment chief Fidji Simo to step down after medical leave
snippet: "Three months ago, I had to go on medical leave after a severe exacerbation of a chronic illness I've lived with for seven years. title: TCS CEO K Krithivasan says AI will not reduce white-collar jobs
snippet: Krithivasan's comments followed the announcement of TCS' Q1 earnings. In the first quarter of FY27, India’s largest software services firm posted a 4.6% on-year growth in net profit at Rs 13,349 crore. Revenue rose 13.9% year-on-year to Rs 72,275 crore.

title: Microsoft emissions surge 27% as AI buildout crimps climate goals
snippet: Microsoft's greenhouse gas emissions jumped 27 percent in its latest fiscal year, the tech giant disclosed Thursday, adding to a wave of worsening environmental reports from an industry racing to build AI infrastructure.
